assessment_items.question_content:
***One of the ways we can identify *Laocoön and his sons* as belonging to the Hellenistic period, is by its stylistic similarity to which important Greek monument?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/abb62a9cef729b647865c5a75979c853.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: ***One of the ways we can identify *Laocoön and his sons* as belonging to the Hellenistic period, is by its stylistic similarity to Altar of Zeus at Pergamon.**

choices.content: the pediment sculptures of the Parthenon
the frieze from the Siphnian Treasury at Delphi
the frieze from the Temple of Athena Nike

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following characteristics of  *Laocoön and his Sons* are typical of the Hellenistic style.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/abb62a9cef729b647865c5a75979c853.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The literary references are used throughout ancient Greek art.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Although *Laocoön and his sons* was found in pieces, archaeologists have been able to reconstruct the original composition.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/abb62a9cef729b647865c5a75979c853.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **What we see today in *Laocoön and his sons* is archaeologists current best guess about the original composition, but is only one of several potential possibilities.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Laocoön and his sons are being attacked by serpents because . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/abb62a9cef729b647865c5a75979c853.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The goddess protecting the Greeks sent serpents to strangle Laocoön (who knew that the wooden horse from the Greeks was full of soldiers) and his sons so that they would not warn the Trojans.**

choices.content: they angered the god protecting the Trojans.
they are engaged in a battle with the Titans.
they knew that the wooden horse from the Greeks was full of soldiers.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which artist was particularly influenced by *Laocoön and his sons*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The expressive power of the body that we see in *Laocoön and his sons* clearly influenced Michelangelo's figures on the Sistine Chapel ceiling.
